title: 不仅...而且 (bùjǐn...érqiě) - Not only...but also
explanation: 不仅...而且 is a more formal version of 不但...而且, used to add emphasis in written and spoken Chinese.

examples.chinese: 他不仅会说英语，而且会说法语。
examples.pinyin: Tā bùjǐn huì shuō Yīngyǔ, érqiě huì shuō Fǎyǔ.
examples.english: He not only speaks English, but also speaks French.

examples.chinese: 这个城市不仅漂亮，而且很安全。
examples.pinyin: Zhège chéngshì bùjǐn piàoliang, érqiě hěn ānquán.
examples.english: This city is not only beautiful, but also very safe.

title: 即使...也 (jíshǐ...yě) - Even if...still
explanation: 即使...也 expresses that even if a condition is true, the result remains unchanged.

examples.pinyin: Jíshǐ xià yǔ, wǒ yě yào qù.
examples.english: Even if it rains, I will still go.

examples.pinyin: Jíshǐ hěn nán, tā yě bú fàngqì.
examples.english: Even if it's hard, he won't give up.

title: 无论...都 (wúlùn...dōu) - No matter...all
explanation: 无论...都 means "no matter what/how/when, the result is always the same."

examples.chinese: 无论多忙，他都坚持锻炼。
examples.pinyin: Wúlùn duō máng, tā dōu jiānchí duànliàn.
examples.english: No matter how busy, he always keeps exercising.

examples.chinese: 无论你去哪儿，我都跟你去。
examples.pinyin: Wúlùn nǐ qù nǎr, wǒ dōu gēn nǐ qù.
examples.english: No matter where you go, I'll go with you.

title: 既...又 (jì...yòu) - Both...and
explanation: 既...又 indicates two qualities or states coexist at the same time.

examples.chinese: 这个餐厅既便宜又好吃。
examples.pinyin: Zhège cāntīng jì piányi yòu hǎochī.
examples.english: This restaurant is both cheap and delicious.

examples.pinyin: Tā jì cōngmíng yòu piàoliang.
examples.english: She is both smart and pretty.

title: 之所以...是因为 (zhīsuǒyǐ...shì yīnwèi)
structure: 之所以 + Result，是因为 + Reason
explanation: 之所以...是因为 states a result first and then explains the reason, meaning "the reason why...is because."

examples.chinese: 他之所以成功，是因为他很努力。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhīsuǒyǐ chénggōng, shì yīnwèi tā hěn nǔlì.
examples.english: The reason he succeeded is because he worked very hard.

examples.chinese: 我之所以迟到，是因为路上堵车了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhīsuǒyǐ chídào, shì yīnwèi lùshang dǔchē le.
examples.english: The reason I was late is because there was a traffic jam.

title: 以...为 (yǐ...wéi) - Take...as
explanation: 以...为 means "to take A as B" or "to regard A as B." It is commonly used in formal contexts.

examples.chinese: 我们以北京为中心开展工作。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yǐ Běijīng wéi zhōngxīn kāizhǎn gōngzuò.
examples.english: We carry out work with Beijing as the center.

examples.pinyin: Tā yǐ jiāo shū wéi lè.
examples.english: He takes pleasure in teaching.

title: Result complement with 得 (de)
explanation: 得 (de) connects a verb to a more detailed result complement describing the extent or outcome of the action.

examples.pinyin: Tā gāoxìng de tiào le qǐlái.
examples.english: He was so happy that he jumped up.

examples.pinyin: Tā lèi de shuō bù chū huà lái.
examples.english: She was so tired she couldn't speak.

title: Extended passive with 被 (bèi)
structure: Subject + 被 + Agent + Verb + Other Elements
explanation: The extended 被 passive can include more complex verb phrases with complements, 了, and other elements.

examples.pinyin: Nà kē shù bèi fēng chuī dǎo le.
examples.english: That tree was blown down by the wind.

examples.chinese: 他的建议被大家接受了。
examples.pinyin: Tā de jiànyì bèi dàjiā jiēshòu le.
examples.english: His suggestion was accepted by everyone.

title: Nominalizer 所 (suǒ)
explanation: 所 (suǒ) is placed before a verb to nominalize it, creating a noun phrase. Often used in formal or written Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Zhè shì wǒ suǒ zhīdào de.
examples.english: This is what I know.

examples.pinyin: Tā suǒ shuō de huà hěn yǒu dàolǐ.
examples.english: What he said makes a lot of sense.

title: Continuous state with 着 (zhe)
explanation: 着 (zhe) indicates a continuous or ongoing state resulting from an action, rather than an action in progress.

examples.pinyin: Mén kāi zhe.
examples.english: The door is open. (state)

examples.pinyin: Qiáng shàng guà zhe yì fú huà.
examples.english: A painting is hanging on the wall.
